title: Traditional Platforms vs Unchained Commerce for Building a Digital Product Platform for the African Creator Economy
author: Unchained Commerce
excerpt: PayPal, Stripe, Gumroad, and Payhip do not work in your country. That is a platform problem, not a you problem. Here is what actually works.

content.text: As a creator in Nigeria, having a digital product is only half the battle - collecting payment is the real challenge when platforms like PayPal and Stripe are not available. This is where Unchained Commerce comes in, offering a solution that bypasses traditional payment processors. With Unchained Commerce, creators can focus on what they do best: producing high-quality digital content. The real solution lies in leveraging crypto-native infrastructure to reach a global audience. By doing so, creators can tap into a vast market, unencumbered by geographic restrictions.
What You Are Actually Comparing

When evaluating traditional platforms versus Unchained Commerce, it's essential to consider the underlying mechanics. Traditional platforms like PayPal and Stripe operate within a complex web of regulatory compliance, fraud risk tiers, and banking infrastructure. In contrast, Unchained Commerce utilizes crypto-native infrastructure, which sidesteps these limitations. This fundamental difference has a significant impact on the ability of creators in restricted countries to sell digital products. By understanding these distinctions, creators can make informed decisions about which platform best suits their needs.
Where the Traditional Platforms Break Down

Traditional platforms like PayPal, Stripe, Gumroad, and Payhip often exclude creators in certain countries due to compliance decisions, fraud risk tiers, and banking infrastructure. This exclusion is particularly pronounced in countries like Nigeria, Ghana, and Kenya, where access to traditional payment processors is limited. As a result, creators in these regions are left without a reliable means of collecting payment for their digital products. This breakdown is not only frustrating but also stifles the growth of the creator economy in these regions.
What Crypto-Native Infrastructure Does Differently

Crypto-native infrastructure, on the other hand, operates without the burden of geographic compliance layers. This means that creators can sell digital products to a global audience, regardless of their location. With crypto-native infrastructure, payment processing is decentralized, and transactions are facilitated through blockchain technology. This approach eliminates the need for traditional payment processors, allowing creators to reach a broader audience and collect payment without restrictions. Unchained Commerce's Core Shop Engine and Web3 Wallet Connect are examples of products that leverage this infrastructure.
Traditional payment platforms like PayPal and Stripe are inaccessible to creators in many countries, stifling the growth of the creator economy.
The Real Cost Difference

While traditional platforms may appear to offer competitive pricing, the true cost difference lies in the restrictions they impose on creators. With traditional platforms, creators in restricted countries often face significant barriers to entry, including high fees, limited access, and stringent compliance requirements. In contrast, Unchained Commerce offers a one-time payment model, with no subscriptions or hidden fees. This transparent pricing model ensures that creators can focus on producing high-quality content, without worrying about the costs associated with selling it.
Which One to Use and Why

For creators in restricted countries, the choice between traditional platforms and Unchained Commerce is clear. Unchained Commerce offers a reliable, crypto-native infrastructure that enables creators to sell digital products to a global audience, without the burden of geographic restrictions. With Unchained Commerce, creators can tap into a vast market, collect payment without limitations, and focus on what they do best: producing high-quality content. By choosing Unchained Commerce, creators can unlock their full potential and participate in the global creator economy.
What Creators in Restricted Countries Are Asking

Creators in restricted countries can get paid for digital products without PayPal by leveraging crypto-native infrastructure. Platforms like Unchained Commerce offer a range of products, including the Core Shop Engine and Web3 Wallet Connect, which enable creators to sell digital products and collect payment through blockchain technology. With these solutions, creators can reach a global audience and collect payment without restrictions, bypassing traditional payment processors altogether.
Crypto-native infrastructure solves this problem by enabling creators to sell digital products and collect payment without restrictions, regardless of their location.
Related Topics Worth Reading

Crypto payments are revolutionizing the creator economy in developing countries, enabling creators to sell digital products and collect payment without restrictions. By leveraging crypto-native infrastructure, creators can tap into a global audience and participate in the global economy, unlocking new opportunities for growth and development.
Your Launch Checklist — No Platform Permission Required

Before launching an Unchained Commerce store, creators should ensure they have the Core Shop Engine set up for automated delivery and payment verification, Web3 Wallet Connect integrated for seamless crypto payments, and the Admin Dashboard configured for real-time analytics and order management. By following this pre-launch checklist, creators can ensure a smooth and successful store launch, and start selling digital products to a global audience.
Success for creators in restricted countries means being able to sell digital products to a global audience, collect payment without limitations, and participate in the global creator economy.
